evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly shows up/down/ns classification with threshold lines and top-5 annotations; directly fulfills brief claim.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set; matplotlib default (likely DejaVu Sans Type-3) used — text will not be selectable/editable in PDF/SVG.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4C72B0) down, red (#DD4949) up, grey (#b0b0b0) ns — directional discipline maintained; blue/red pair is distinguishable for most colorblind types.
evidence.layout_density: 7x5.5 inches with tight_layout; scatter points fill the panel, legend upper-left, annotations offset with arrows — no gaping whitespace or overlaps.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure: exact counts, threshold values, axis labels, annotation rationale; legend is clearly placed; a reviewer would find no obvious discrepancies.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 is not set in the script, so fonts are embedded as non-selectable Type-3 bitmaps in the PDF; svg.fonttype='none' also absent.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=3000, gives numerical counts (up=235, down=230, ns=2535), explains axes, thresholds (|log2FC|>=1, p<=0.01), and annotation methodology.

overall_strengths: Strong scientific content: clear 3-class volcano with threshold lines and top-5 annotation fulfills the brief completely.
Excellent caption with specific numerical findings (counts, threshold values) and honest annotation caveat about index labels.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none', so text is not selectable/editable in the vector outputs — a critical omission for publication.
No explicit font family set; matplotlib default (DejaVu Sans) is used rather than a publication-quality sans-serif (e.g. Arial, Helvetica, or a system sans).
